assumptions: The question concerns consolidated financial statements under IFRS.
The dividend has already been declared and a corresponding intragroup receivable/payable has been recognised.
The issue is limited to hedge accounting models relevant to foreign currency risk.

approaches.label: Fair value hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 and IFRS 9 identify fair value hedge accounting as a distinct hedge accounting model for recognised assets or liabilities exposed to a particular risk that could affect profit or loss.

approaches.label: Cash flow hedge
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39 identifies cash flow hedge accounting as a separate hedge accounting model for exposures to variability in cash flows attributable to a particular risk on recognised items or highly probable forecast transactions.

approaches.label: Hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation
approaches.rationale_for_inclusion: IAS 39, IFRS 9 and IFRIC 16 identify hedge of a net investment in a foreign operation as a distinct hedge accounting model for foreign currency risk.
